recalls.Description: Isla Rae magnetic wireless chargers have been recalled due to potential fire and burn hazards. These chargers, designed to magnetically attach to compatible phones, were sold in white, pink, and purple colors. The model number 'RM5PBM' is located on the side of the charger, below the specifications '5000 mAh 3.7V.' It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly to avoid fire risks, as they cannot be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins. Check with your local hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal methods.
recalls.Title: Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Chargers Recalled for Fire and Burn Risks; Available at T.J. Maxx and Marshalls
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach TJX at 888-256-1564 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email powerbank@realtimeresults.net, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/powerbank. Additionally, Marshalls and T.J. Maxx customers can find recall details on their respective websites under 'Product Recalls.'

Products.Name: Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 13,200 (In addition, about 7,000 units were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (white)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (pink)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger (purple)
Recalled Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Charger Model: RM5PBM

Retailers.Name: Available at Marshalls and T.J. Maxx stores across the country from June 2024 to November 2025 for $15.

Importers.Name: Hello to Green dba Press Play Products of Bell, California

Distributors.Name: The TJX Companies Inc., of Framingham, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of explosion during use, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled chargers immediately and register at https://www.recallrtr.com/powerbank for a full refund. Dispose of the charger according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Isla Rae magnetic wireless chargers recalled due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a refund and dispose of the chargers safely.

recalls.Description: INIU is recalling its 10,000mAh portable power banks, model BI-B41, due to potential fire and burn hazards. These power banks are identifiable by their black or blue casing, featuring the INIU logo and a paw-print LED light on the front. The affected units have specific serial numbers: 000G21, 000H21, 000I21, and 000L21, which can be found printed on the back of the device. It is crucial to handle these lithium-ion batteries carefully, as improper disposal can lead to fire risks. Municipal hazardous waste centers may accept these devices for safe disposal, but consumers should confirm with their local facility beforehand.
recalls.Title: INIU Recalls 10,000mAh Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks; Available on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach INIU at 888-886-3606 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email recall@iniu.shop. More information is available online at https://iniushop.com/pages/recall-b41# or by visiting https://iniushop.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the top.

Images.Caption: Recalled INIU Power Bank – model BI-B41

Injuries.Name: INIU has received 15 reports of power banks overheating, including 11 reports of fires that resulted in three minor burn injuries and property damage totaling over $380,000.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Topstar Industry Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at Amazon.com between August 2021 and April 2022 for approximately $18.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the power banks and contact INIU for disposal guidance.

recalls.metaDescription: INIU recalls 10,000mAh power banks due to fire risks. Check your model and serial number for safety.

recalls.Description: Belkin has issued a recall for specific models of portable power banks and wireless charging stands due to potential fire and burn hazards. The affected charging stand is identified by model number MMA008, while the power banks are labeled as BPB002 and PB0003. Serial numbers starting with '57X' for the charging stand and '35S' for the BPB002 power bank are included in this recall. All serial numbers for the PB0003 power bank are also affected. The charging stands and BPB002 power banks were sold in black, while the PB0003 was available in both black and white.
recalls.Title: Belkin Recalls Portable Power Banks and Wireless Charging Stands Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Belkin at 800-223-5546 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information can be found online at http://www.belkin.com/MMA008recall and http://www.belkin.com/BPB002recall, or by visiting https://www.belkin.com/ and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Wireless Charging Stand MMA008
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 83,500 (In addition, about 2,385 were sold in Canada)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a fire incident in the U.S. (and 15 reports internationally). Two minor burn injuries and property damage totaling $37,765 have been reported outside of the U.S.

Manufacturers.Name: Belkin International Inc., of El Segundo, California

Retailers.Name: Best Buy, Target, Walmart, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Belkin.com, Amazon.com, and TikTok from August 2020 through August 2025, priced between $29 and $180.

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion battery posing fire and burn risks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products and contact Belkin for a full refund or store credit.

recalls.metaDescription: Belkin recalls portable power banks and wireless charging stands due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: The recall now includes the ESR HaloLock wireless power banks with model number 2G505, alongside previously recalled models 2G520, 2G505B, and 2G512B. Each power bank features 'ESR' on the back and model numbers on the right side. Model 2G505 is available in black and white, while the other models come in various colors including dark blue, light blue, gray, white, and black. All recalled units have five circular LED lights on one side.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using these power banks immediately and reach out to Waymeet at 888-990-0280 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. You can also email support@esrtech.com or visit https://www.esrtech.com/pages/recall for more information.

Products.Name: ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 19,500 (In addition, about 2,015 were sold in Canada). Waymeet previously recalled 24,000 power banks on August 14, 2025 (In addition, 9,900 were sold in Canada that were covered in the August recall)

Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G520
Recalled ESR HaloLock Kickstand Wireless Power Bank Model 2G505B
Recalled ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Bank Model 2G512B

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 20 reports of the lithium-ion battery catching fire and exploding, resulting in property damage of about $30,000. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com (all models), Esrtech.com (all models), and Homedepot.com (models 2G520, 2G505B, and 2G512B only) from September 2022 to July 2025, priced between $32 and $50.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Waymeet for a full refund. Provide a photo of the power bank with 'Recalled' marked on it. Dispose of safely according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall of ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks due to fire risks. Stop using and contact Waymeet for a refund.

recalls.Description: Zyntony has announced a recall of its Kogalla-branded power banks, specifically models BP125, BatPak 2F, and BatPak 3F. These rechargeable USB power banks, equipped with lithium-ion batteries of 6,700mAh, 13,400mAh, or 20,100mAh, were sold individually and as part of Kogalla trail light kits. The Kogalla logo is displayed on the front, while the model number can be found on the back. The BP125 model features a black USB-A jack, whereas the BatPak 2F and 3F models come with orange USB-A jacks. It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly to avoid fire hazards. Do not discard them in regular trash or recycling bins; instead, check with your local hazardous waste collection center for safe disposal options.
recalls.Title: Zyntony Issues Recall for Kogalla Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Kogalla via email at bpreplace@kogalla.com or visit https://rtn.kogalla.com/pages/kogalla-recalls-batpak1-batpak-2f-batpak-3f. Alternatively, visit https://kogalla.com/ and click on “Recall Information” at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Kogalla rechargeable USB power banks - front
Recalled Kogalla rechargeable USB power banks - back

Injuries.Name: Zyntony has received two reports of lithium-ion battery overheating and catching fire, even when not in use, resulting in one minor burn injury and property damage, totaling about $3,300.

Retailers.Name: Available on Kogalla.com from June 2024 to January 2025 for BP125 and BatPak 2F power banks, and from August 2024 to January 2025 for BatPak 3F power banks, with prices ranging from $45 to $90 for the power banks and $180 to $230 for the trail lights sold with them.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition from lithium-ion batteries, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled power banks and contact Kogalla for a free replacement, including shipping. They will need to complete the online form at https://rtn.kogalla.com/products/request-batpak-replacement and dispose of the recalled power banks according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Zyntony recalls Kogalla power banks due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact Kogalla for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: CT-ENERGY has issued a recall for its Lithium Coin Battery Chargers, specifically model "nc-02." This product includes a charger, four rechargeable 2032 lithium-ion batteries, and a USB cable. The brand name "CT-ENERGY" is displayed on the front, while the model number is located on the back of the device. The recall is due to serious safety concerns regarding the accessibility of lithium coin batteries by children, which can lead to severe injuries or fatalities if ingested. The packaging does not meet child-resistant requirements as mandated by safety regulations.
recalls.Title: CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Chargers Recalled for Safety Risks Related to Battery Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to CT New Energy Technology via email at info@ct-energy.cn, or visit their website at www.ct-energy.cn/about/22.html?land=en-us. Click on "Urgent Product Safety Recall Notice" at the top of the page for additional details.

Products.Name: CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Charger Set

Images.Caption: Recalled CT-ENERGY Lithium Coin Battery Charger with Rechargeable 2032 Batteries

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from March 2024 to May 2025 for approximately $17.

Distributors.Name: CT (Foshan) New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. (dba CT-Energy), of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from battery ingestion due to non-child-resistant packaging and accessibility of batteries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the charger, remove batteries, and contact for a full refund or replacement kit.

recalls.metaDescription: CT-ENERGY recalls Lithium Coin Battery Chargers due to serious safety risks. Learn how to get a refund or replacement kit.

recalls.Description: ESR HaloLock wireless power banks, identified by model numbers 2G520, 2G505B, and 2G512B, are being recalled due to safety concerns. These compact power banks, available in dark blue, light blue, gray, white, and black, feature five circular LED display lights on one side and have 'ESR' printed on the back along with the model number on the right side. The lithium-ion batteries in these devices can overheat, leading to potential fire and burn hazards.
recalls.Title: Recall of ESR HaloLock Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled power banks immediately and reach out to Waymeet for a full refund. Contact them toll-free at 888-990-0280 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email support@esrtech.com, or visit https://www.esrtech.com/pages/recall. You can also go to www.esrtech.com and select 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

recalls.Description: The recall affects the iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank, a portable charger with a 5000 mAh capacity, specifically the IST-09991/W05 model. These power banks feature the iStore logo on the front and the model number on the back. Measuring approximately 3 inches by 2 inches by 0.25 inches and weighing around 3.2 ounces, they were sold with a 12-inch USB-C to USB-C cable. It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly to prevent fire hazards. Do not discard them in regular trash or recycling bins; instead, contact your local household hazardous waste collection center for guidance on safe disposal.
recalls.Title: Recall of iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact iStore at 800-268-4049 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@istore.co, or visit https://istore.co/pages/battery-recall and https://www.tomauri.com/pages/battery-recall. You can also find more information by visiting http://www.tomauri.com and clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (Model IST-09991/W05)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8,520 (An additional 6,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (packaging)
Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (front)
Recalled iStore Magnetic Wireless Power Bank (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the wireless power banks overheating and exploding while they were charging, including one incident that resulted in a minor burn to the victim's arm. The firm received reports of property damage of about $15,000.

Retailers.Name: Available through vending machines and retail locations at various airports across the country, as well as online at iStore.co, from February 2023 to May 2025, priced between $46 and $65.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition of the lithium-ion battery during charging, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease usage of the recalled power bank and visit the company's website to initiate the recall process. Participants will need to email two photos to recall@istore.co: one showing the front of the power bank with 'Recalled' and the date written on it, and another displaying the model number on the back. A purchase receipt is not mandatory for recall participation. Proper disposal of the power banks should follow local hazardous waste regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: iStore recalls Magnetic Wireless Power Banks due to fire hazards. Learn how to participate in the recall and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description: Anker Innovations has issued a recall for the Anker PowerCore 10000 power banks, specifically model number A1263. These power banks feature the brand name 'Anker' on the front, with the model number and serial number printed on the bottom. Only those power banks sold in the U.S. with specific serial numbers are affected. Consumers can verify if their device is part of the recall by visiting https://www.anker.com/product-recalls. It is crucial not to dispose of these recalled lithium-ion batteries in regular trash or recycling streams due to their fire hazard. Instead, check with local household hazardous waste centers for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Over One Million Anker PowerCore 10000 Power Banks Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Anker at 800-988-7973 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at support@anker.com. Additional support is available online at https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus, through a live chat at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us, or by visiting https://www.anker.com/product-recalls.

Images.Caption: Recalled Anker A1263 power bank

Injuries.Name: Anker has received 19 reports of fires and explosions. This includes two reports of minor burn injuries not requiring medical attention and 11 reports of property damage totaling over $60,700.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Anker, Amazon, Newegg, and eBay from June 2016 to December 2022, priced around $27.

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion battery posing fire and burn risks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Anker for a free replacement. Registration for the recall can be done at https://www.anker.com/product-recalls. A photo of the recalled power bank showing the model number, serial number, and the word 'recalled' written on it will be required, along with a confirmation of proper disposal.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Over one million Anker PowerCore 10000 power banks recalled for fire risks. Check if yours is affected and learn how to get a replacement.

recalls.Description:
iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks have been recalled due to potential fire and burn hazards. These pocket-sized power banks, which feature the 'iWALK' logo on the front and model numbers on the back, are compatible with various devices and come in multiple colors. All recalled units have a circular LED display indicating the remaining battery life. Specific models affected include the DBL6000M and DBL6000P, which also have a circular phone holder ring above the display. A complete list of model numbers and serial numbers is provided for identification.

Consumers are advised not to dispose of these lithium-ion batteries in regular trash or recycling bins, as they pose a significant fire risk. Instead, check with your local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options. It’s crucial to contact the center beforehand to confirm their acceptance of recalled lithium-ion batteries.
recalls.Title: Recall of iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to U2O Global Co. Ltd. at 888-279-0111 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at service@iwalk.net, or visit https://www.iwalk.net/recall.html. You can also find more information by going to www.iwalk.net and clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Recalled iWALK Power Bank Model MXB008 (back)

Recalled iWALK Power Bank Models DBL6000M/DBL6000P (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the lithium-ion battery catching fire, resulting in 10 burn injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: U2O Global Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available online at iWalkMall.com, Amazon.com, and various e-commerce platforms from July 2021 to March 2024, priced between $20 and $57.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power banks and contact U2O Global for a full refund. To process your refund, email a photo of the power bank showing the model and serial number, with 'Recalled' marked on it, to U2O Global. Follow local hazardous waste disposal guidelines for the recalled units.

recalls.metaDescription: iWALK Magnetic Wireless Power Banks recalled due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact U2O Global for a refund.

recalls.Description: Baseus has issued a recall for its 65W 30000mAh portable chargers, identified by model number BS-30KP365 and serial numbers ending in 0-9 or the letter 'D'. These chargers, available in black and white, feature '65w' on the front and a label on the bottom with the brand name, model number, and serial number starting with 'SN'. It's crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries properly due to their fire risk. Do not discard them in regular trash or recycling bins. Check with your local hazardous waste center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Baseus 65W Portable Chargers Recalled for Fire Risk; Available on Amazon and Other Retailers
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Baseus toll-free at 888-568-7216 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email care@baseus.com, or visit https://www.baseus.com/pages/recall_select_BS-30KP365 for more details.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Baseus Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled chargers and contact Baseus for a free replacement. The new charger will have the same model number but a different serial number ending in 'A'.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Baseus 65W portable chargers pose a fire hazard. Learn how to get a free replacement and dispose of your charger safely.

recalls.Description: Casely is recalling its Power Pods, a 5000mAh portable MagSafe wireless phone charger, due to potential fire and burn hazards. These pocket-sized chargers, marked with 'Casely' on the front and model number E33A on the back, are compatible with a variety of devices. They come in multiple colors and prints, measuring approximately 3 x 2 x 0.25 inches and weighing about 3.2 ounces. It's crucial to handle the recalled lithium-ion batteries properly to avoid fire risks; do not dispose of them in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, check with your local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Casely Recalls Power Pods Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Casely toll-free at 888-964-9331 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email recall@getcasely.com, or visit https://www.getcasely.com/pages/2025-recall. More information is also available at www.getcasely.com under the 'Recalls' section.

Recalled Casely wireless portable power bank (model number)

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to Charmast power banks, specifically model W1056, available in various colors including black, blue, green, mint, pink, and white. These power banks feature the brand name 'Charmast' on the front and 'Model: W1056' on the back. It is crucial to note that recalled lithium-ion batteries must be disposed of according to local and state regulations, as they require special handling compared to regular batteries. Do not discard these recalled power banks in the trash or standard recycling bins.
recalls.Title: Recall of Charmast Power Banks Model W1056 Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Charmast at 929-636-0293, Monday through Friday, from 10 a.m. to 3 p.m. ET, via email at rcus@charmast.com, or visit https://www.charmast.com/pages/recall-info-page. Alternatively, visit www.charmast.com and click on 'Recalls' for further details.

Recalled Charmast W1056 power bank in black (back view)

Injuries.Name: Charmast has received 44 reports of the recalled power banks expanding, igniting, melting, overheating or smoking, including four reports of consumers receiving burns or blisters.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from December 2018 to September 2024, priced between $14 and $25.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Charmast Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Charmast Power Banks Model W1056 due to fire hazards. Contact Charmast for a refund and safe disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: HTRC C240 battery chargers, designed for charging lithium-ion batteries, have been recalled due to serious fire and burn hazards. These chargers are black with dual blue screens labeled 1 and 2, and feature the model identifier 'C240 DUO' between the screens, along with the brand name 'HTRC' located in the bottom left corner. Consumers are advised to take this recall seriously as it poses risks of severe injury or even death.
recalls.Title: HTRC C240 Battery Chargers Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach HTRC at 951-215-8389 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, email servicesupport@ht-rc.com, or visit www.ht-rc.com/recalls.asp. Additional information can also be found by clicking on 'Recalls' at the top of the homepage.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to potential ignition

recalls.metaDescription: HTRC recalls C240 battery chargers due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund.

The serial number, also found on the back, consists of 14 digits starting with '57V'. These chargers, available in black, feature a white magnetic charger specifically designed for smart watches, with 'Belkin' printed on the front in white.

recalls.Description: Baseus is recalling specific models of its magnetic wireless charging power banks, identified as PPCXM06 and PPCXW06. These power banks are available in several colors including white, black, light blue, and light pink. Each model features a magnetic side designed for attachment to mobile devices that support magnetic charging. The model number is printed on the magnetic side, while '20W' is noted on the non-magnetic side. The PPCXM06 measures approximately 4 inches by 2.5 inches by 0.64 inches, and the PPCXW06 measures about 4 inches by 2.5 inches by 0.5 inches. Only these specific models are included in the recall, although some units may have been listed on Amazon under a different model number, PPXCW06.
recalls.Title: Recall of Baseus Magnetic Wireless Charging Power Banks Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach Baseus at 855-215-5824 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at care@baseus.com. Additional information is available at https://baseuspowerbankrecall.expertinquiry.com or by visiting www.baseus.com and selecting 'Product Recall Information' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Baseus Magnetic Wireless Charging Power Bank PPCXM06

Images.Caption: Recalled Magnetic Wireless Charging Power Banks
Recalled PPCXM06 Magnetic Power Bank
Recalled PPCXW06 Power Bank

Injuries.Name: Baseus has received 171 reports of incidents, including 132 reports of bulging or swelling batteries and 39 reports of fires, resulting in 13 burn injuries and about $20,000 in property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, AliExpress.com, and Baseus.com from April 2022 through April 2024, priced between $18 and $55.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Baseus Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power banks and contact Baseus for a full refund with proof of purchase, or a $36 cash refund without proof of purchase. A photo showing the model number must be submitted via the recall registration page. For those without an order number, include a photo of the power bank with your name and date written in permanent marker. Baseus and Amazon are notifying all known purchasers directly. Dispose of the battery according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Baseus recalls magnetic wireless charging power banks due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: myCharge is recalling its POWER HUB All-In-One 10,000mAh portable chargers due to potential fire and burn hazards. The affected chargers, available in black, come with two built-in cables for charging devices and retractable wall prongs for recharging the unit. The model numbers involved in this recall are AO10FK-A, AO10FK-B, and AO10FK-C. A date code, formatted as a 4-digit number (WWYY), is printed on the wall prong, with the recalled codes ending in 21, 22, or specific codes from 2023: 0123, 0923, 1523, or 1723.
recalls.Title: myCharge Recalls POWER HUB All-In-One Portable Chargers Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled chargers and reach out to myCharge at 888-251-2026 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at compliance@mycharge.com, or visit https://mycharge.com/pages/power-hub-all-in-one-recall for further information.

Products.Name: myCharge POWER HUB All-In-One 10,000mAh Portable Charger

Images.Caption: Recalled myCharge POWER HUB All-In-One 10,000mAh portable charger, models AO10FK-A, AO10FK-B, and AO10FK-C
Location of WWYY date code printed on wall prong

Injuries.Name: There have been a total of 120 reports involving the portable chargers overheating. myCharge received five reports of incidents, including two reports of residential fires, resulting in approximately $165,000 in reported property damage; and Costco received 115 returns of the portable chargers, mentioning melting, expanding, smoking, fire, burning, exploding, or sparking. There have been no reports of injuries requiring medical attention.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at Costco locations and on Costco.com from January 2022 through November 2023 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: RFA Brands LLC, d/b/a myCharge, of Birmingham, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating during charging, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the chargers and contact myCharge for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: myCharge recalls POWER HUB portable chargers due to fire hazards. Stop using them and contact myCharge for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Vernier Science Education has issued a recall for the Go Direct Charge Station, which is designed to charge the Go Direct line of wireless sensors that utilize rechargeable batteries. These sensors are commonly employed in educational environments for various scientific experiments, including data collection on temperature, oxygen, carbon dioxide, and pH levels. The Go Direct Charge Station can charge up to 16 sensors simultaneously—8 through USB and 8 via wand-style sensor ports. This product is labeled as 'Go Direct® Charging Station' and can be identified by the order code GDX-CRG and model number GDXCRG2617R1, located on a label at the bottom of the unit. It may have been sold individually or included in Teacher Packs with order codes GDX-TMP-TP or GDX-PH-TP.
recalls.Title: Vernier Science Education Recalls Go Direct Charge Stations Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are advised to stop using the Vernier Go Direct Charge Station immediately. For further assistance, contact Vernier Science Education at 888-837-6437, available from 6:45 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Thursday and 6:45 a.m. to 3 p.m. PT on Friday. You can also email support@vernier.com or visit www.vernier.com/gdx-crg-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vernier Go Direct Charge Station
Images.SourceImageURL: https://www.cpsc.gov/s3fs-public/Recalled Vernier Go Direct Charge Stations.png

Images.Caption: Label located at the bottom of the charge ctation
Images.SourceImageURL: https://www.cpsc.gov/s3fs-public/Label located at the bottom of the Charge Station.png

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the chargers overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Vernier.com, Fishersci.com, Flinnsci.com, and Us.vwr.com from November 2017 to December 2023, priced between $90 and $930 based on individual or Teacher Pack purchases.

Importers.Name: Vernier Science Education, of Beaverton, Oregon

Remedies.Name: Stop using the charge station and contact Vernier for return instructions and free repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Vernier recalls Go Direct Charge Stations due to burn hazards. Stop using and contact for repair instructions.

recalls.Description: IKEA has issued a recall for its ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Chargers due to potential burn and electric shock hazards. These chargers, which include a detachable power cord with a C7 connector, have the model number ICPSW5-40-1 printed on the label located on the back of the gray charger. Over time, the charger's cable may become damaged, increasing the risk of injury.
recalls.Title: IKEA Recalls ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Chargers Due to Fire and Electric Shock Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact IKEA toll-free at 888-966-4532, available from 9 a.m. to 10 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at www.ikea.com/us/en/customer-service/product-support/recalls/ or by visiting www.ikea.com/us/en and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Charger
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 22,600 (In addition, about 10,250 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Charger
The product can be identified by the model number ICPSW5-40-1, which can be found on the label on the back side of the USB charger

Injuries.Name: IKEA has received 17 incident reports globally, including five reports of burns and four reports of electric shock. No incidents or injuries have been reported in the U.S.

Retailers.Name: Available at IKEA stores across the country and online at IKEA.com from April 2020 through December 2023, priced around $30.

Distributors.Name: IKEA US Retail LLC, of Virginia

Remedies.Name: Stop using the chargers and return them to any IKEA store for a full refund. If returning in-store is not possible, contact IKEA for disposal instructions to receive a full refund. No proof of purchase is required.

recalls.metaDescription: IKEA recalls ÅSKSTORM 40W USB Chargers due to burn and electric shock hazards. Stop using immediately and return for a full refund.
